data.title: As a DSO plugin developer, I can query the UI/UX reference corpus through a retrieval script and get relevant tagged entries back
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Creates the foundational infrastructure for the UI/UX reference library — corpus directory structure, YAML frontmatter schema, pre-commit validation hook and its registration, plugin boundary allowlist entry, manifest, retrieval script with full SC3 flags, shim registration, and provenance tracking file. Includes 3-5 seed entries to validate end-to-end retrieval.

**Why**: All five downstream integration epics depend on ref-query.sh existing and the corpus schema being established. This walking skeleton proves the retrieval concept works and unblocks all corpus population stories.

**Scope**:
- IN: plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/ directory; _schema.yaml (tag vocabulary with recommended values per domain); check-corpus-schema.sh pre-commit hook + .pre-commit-config.yaml registration + plugins/dso/data/** boundary allowlist; _index.yaml (manually authored, seed entries); plugins/dso/scripts/ref-query.sh (stdlib Python BM25, --top-n/--tier/--session-hash flags, 500-line output limit, zero-result stderr signal, 10s target); dso ref-query shim; docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml; unit tests
- OUT: Full corpus population (Stories 2-5b), integration tests (Story-6), downstream skill integration (epics 2-6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/ exists with _schema.yaml defining all six tag dimension vocabularies (domain, component, action, compliance, severity, story_type) including specific recommended values for common content types; ≥3 domain subdirectories with ≥3 seed entries each having valid YAML frontmatter and ### Summary / ### Detail / ### Implementation tiers
  ← Satisfies: "corpus directory at plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/ with domain-partitioned Markdown files, YAML frontmatter per entry, and three Markdown resolution tiers. _schema.yaml + check-corpus-schema.sh pre-commit hook enforce tag vocabulary." (SC1)

- When this story is complete, check-corpus-schema.sh is registered in .pre-commit-config.yaml with file filter ^plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/ and timeout wrapper; running it against a valid entry passes and against an entry with an invalid tag value fails with the offending field identified
  ← Satisfies: SC1 (hook must actually execute on commit, not just exist)

- When this story is complete, plugins/dso/data/** is added to .claude/hooks/pre-commit/plugin-boundary-allowlist.conf; running the boundary check against staged corpus files passes with no rejection
  ← Satisfies: SC1 (prerequisite for any corpus commit to succeed)

- When this story is complete, _index.yaml is committed to the repository listing all seed entries with all required fields (id, title, domain[], component[], action[], compliance[], severity, story_type[], coverage, source, license, file_path, one-liner); check-corpus-schema.sh --validate-index confirms all entries resolve to valid files
  ← Satisfies: "_index.yaml manifest committed to the repository (not generated), listing every entry with six-dimension tags, one-liner, file path, coverage tier, source, and license." (SC2)

- When this story is complete, plugins/dso/scripts/ref-query.sh accepts a query string and returns correctly formatted Markdown entries within 10 seconds; --top-n (default 8), --tier=summary|detail|implementation, and --session-hash flags work as specified; output is truncated to 500 lines maximum with a stderr warning on truncation; zero-result query emits [ref-query: no results for query: "..." with filters: ...] to stderr and exits 0
  ← Satisfies: "plugins/dso/scripts/ref-query.sh retrieval script using stdlib Python BM25 (self-contained, no external deps)..." (SC3)

- When this story is complete, dso ref-query [args] correctly invokes ref-query.sh via the .claude/scripts/dso shim; check-shim-refs.sh does not block downstream skill file references to dso ref-query
  ← Satisfies: "ref-query.sh registered as dso ref-query shim" (SC6)

- When this story is complete, docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml is committed at the project root with entries for all planned corpus sources (name, URL, type, pinned version/commit, last-synced, license, domains)
  ← Satisfies: "docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml file at the project root tracks each external reference source" (SC7)

- When this story is complete, unit tests pass covering: BM25 scoring logic (mock corpus fixture), schema validation logic (valid + invalid frontmatter), manifest integrity check, and shim invocation
  ← Satisfies: TDD unit test done-of-done requirement

## Considerations

- [Performance] BM25 re-reads corpus on each call — 10s target must be verified at full corpus scale (~500+ entries after Stories 2-5b complete); benchmark in integration test
- [Reliability] check-corpus-schema.sh must fail-open gracefully if Python unavailable (must not block git commits)
- [Security] ref-query.sh query string must not be passed to shell unquoted — use Python argument parsing, not eval/exec/shell interpolation
- [Testing] BM25 unit tests must use a mock corpus fixture (not live corpus files) so tests pass before corpus population is complete
- [Reliability] ref-query.sh must emit a clear stderr error (not Python stack trace) and exit with a documented non-zero code when python3 is unavailable

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
